behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert specific details not mentioned in instruction.md: (1) test_godoclint_enabled_in_config checks for the literal string 'godoclint' as the linter name, but instruction.md only says 'Enable a godoc linter' without naming 'godoclint' specifically. (2) Tests check specific files local_unix.go and local_windows.go for the exact string '// Package local provides the default implementation for volumes', but instruction.md never names these specific files. (3) test_local_package_has_proper_do

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md describes what's broken (duplicate package documentation) and what to do (resolve duplicates, enable a godoc linter) without specifying exact lines to change or exact code replacements.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_godoclint_enabled_in_config asserts the exact linter name 'godoclint' and checks it appears in config['linters']['enable']. An agent could reasonably enable a different godoc-related linter (e.g., 'godot'). Also test_local_package_has_proper_doc asserts '// Package local provides' must be in local.go — an agent might place canonical docs in a doc.go file instead, which is a valid Go convention.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: While some tests do text-based checks (checking file contents for duplicate doc strings, checking .golangci.yml for godoclint), several tests execute real behavior: test_go_code_compiles runs 'go build', test_go_vet runs 'go vet', test_go_fmt runs 'go fmt', and test_local_unit_tests runs 'go test'. Not every test is purely text-based.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for absence of duplicate doc comments in local_unix.go/local_windows.go and presence of 'godoclint' in .golangci.yml. A stub/no-op solution wouldn't pass these since the base commit has duplicates present and godoclint not enabled.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/moby's container at solution/solve.sh. The agent could read it directly (cat /solution/solve.sh or similar). The Dockerfile doesn't remove or protect the solution directory. Also the test file itself at tests/test_outputs.py reveals exact assertions (literal strings like 'godoclint', specific file paths) that could be pattern-matched.

behavior_in_tests.reason: instruction.md requires: (1) resolve duplicate package docs — tested by test_unix_file_no_package_doc and test_windows_file_no_package_doc; (2) enable godoc linter in .golangci.yml — tested by test_godoclint_enabled_in_config; (3) go build/vet/fmt pass — tested by respective tests. All stated requirements are covered.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_godoclint_enabled_in_config, test_unix_file_no_package_doc, etc.), have docstrings indicating fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass, and the module docstring explains the PR context.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task doesn't produce structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md says to audit 'all Go source files in daemon/volume/local/' without naming the specific buggy files (local_unix.go, local_windows.go). It describes the problem generically.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold patch makes a localized edit to .golangci.yml (adding one line for godoclint, minor comment fix) and removes doc comments from two files. Changes are minimal and don't contradict other files.
